question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that buildAndValidateFilters() processes all query parameters before any database interaction, and by sending requests with the maximum number of repeated parameters cause CPU exhaustion in the validation layer itself, contributing to a partition-like outage before even reaching the database?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with timestamp=gte:0 and order=desc, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to scan from the latest block backwards with no upper bound, performing a large sequential scan, and by flooding with such requests at high concurrency cause database connection pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/:hashOrNumber with a block number string that is exactly 19 digits long (the maximum allowed by positiveLongRegex), causing isPositiveLong() to parse it as a BigInt and pass it to RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber(), and by flooding with such requests cause database connection pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the GET /blocks route does not enforce any per-IP or per-session request quotas to send a sustained high-volume flood that causes the Node.js process to run out of file descriptors (open socket connections), causing the mirror node to stop accepting new connections and become effectively partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gte:1 and block.number=lte:1 (a single specific block) at extremely high concurrency, causing many simultaneous identical index lookups that collectively exhaust the database connection pool and cause a partition-like outage for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/:hashOrNumber with a hash value that is a valid 64-hex-char string of alternating characters (e.g., 'ababab...'), causing the LIKE query to scan the hash index inefficiently, and by flooding with such requests cause database I/O exhaustion and a partition-like outage?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the blocksQuery selects the bytes field indirectly via `coalesce(size, length(bytes))`, which may cause the database to read large TOAST-stored byte values from disk for every row, causing excessive disk I/O under maximum-limit requests and contributing to a partition-like outage?
